mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8% within a single month, it signals a labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, depressing equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in retail sales indicates a demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint IC of INDPRO and RSAFS remains above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, aggressive Fed rate hikes fail to cool demand, causing real rates to spike and equity multiples to contract sharply over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation tightening periods are not significantly negative compared to low-inflation periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity, supporting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp slowdown in business investment and consumer confidence, triggering a negative equity reaction over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns following housing start drops below 1.2M are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth exceeds 200k while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, it indicates a surge in labor force participation outpacing job creation, which historically precedes earnings revisions downward and negative equity returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS changes and UNRATE changes yields a negative coefficient with p < 0.05 in a forward regression, or forward returns are not depressed.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the Fed funds rate exceeds 4.0%, a sustained decline in industrial production accelerates inventory destocking, compressing profit margins and driving negative equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines in high-rate environments do not produce statistically significant negative equity returns (t-test p > 0.05).
